assertions.expectation: Identifies Service A (minAvailable=100%) as a guaranteed upgrade blocker — no disruptions allowed at all
assertions.reason: Response clearly identifies Service A as blocking with ❌ symbol and states 'Will block ALL node upgrades indefinitely' due to 100% availability requirement

assertions.expectation: Identifies Service B (maxUnavailable=0) as also blocking upgrades — same effect as 100% minAvailable
assertions.reason: Response identifies Service B as blocking with ❌ and explicitly states 'maxUnavailable=0 — BLOCKING' and 'Identical to 100% availability — will block upgrades'

assertions.expectation: States Service C (minAvailable=2 with 3 replicas) is safe — allows 1 pod disruption at a time
assertions.reason: Response marks Service C with ✅ SAFE and states '3 replicas - 2 required = 1 pod can be unavailable during drain'

assertions.expectation: States Service D (maxUnavailable=1 with 5 replicas) is safe — allows 1 pod down at a time
assertions.reason: Response marks Service D with ✅ SAFE and states 'allows 1 pod eviction at a time' and 'Maintains 4/5 pods (80% capacity) during drain'

assertions.expectation: Recommends fixing Service A: change to minAvailable=N-1 or maxUnavailable=1
assertions.reason: Response provides specific fix with kubectl patch commands changing to maxUnavailable=1 and alternative of minAvailable=90%

assertions.expectation: Recommends fixing Service B: change to maxUnavailable=1
assertions.reason: Response provides specific fix with kubectl patch command: 'kubectl patch pdb service-b-pdb -p '{"spec":{"maxUnavailable":1}}'

assertions.expectation: Explains that GKE respects PDBs for up to 1 hour during surge upgrades, then may force-drain
assertions.reason: Response states 'GKE respects PDBs for up to 1 hour during node upgrades, then force-evicts remaining pods' in the introduction

assertions.expectation: Provides kubectl patch commands to fix the problematic PDBs
assertions.reason: Response provides specific kubectl patch commands for both Service A and Service B, along with additional examples in the immediate actions section

assertions.expectation: Recommends testing PDB changes in a non-production environment first
assertions.reason: While the response provides testing commands to verify PDB configurations, it does not explicitly recommend testing changes in a non-production environment first
